title: The Same Ban Passes in Valmiria — This Time Nobody Argues About Whether the Charter Applies, in a Union All Twenty-Seven Had to Build
setting:
This is Slavno again: the same river town in the north of Valmiria, the same disused textile warehouse behind the chain-link fence on Kestenova, the same Emine Sabitova — hospital midwife, chair of the Slavno Muslim Foundation, her congregation of three hundred still ducking the pipes in the trade-union basement. The National Line bloc, the Act on the Suspension of the Establishment of New Places of Worship drafted as neutral heritage policy, the poll at sixty-one percent, the stop-work order pinned to the fence within three weeks — every detail is identical to the run that strained. Nothing about the attack has changed. Nothing about the town, the people, the Act, or the rights at stake has changed.

One thing has changed, and it is not in Valmiria. This is a European Union that, roughly a decade before these events, did the thing the real Union has never done: it convened a Convention, obtained the agreement of every one of its member governments, and carried ratification in all twenty-seven — to amend one sentence of primary law. Article 51 no longer confines the Charter to the Member States 'only when they are implementing Union law'; in this Union the Charter binds a Member State in everything it does. The road not taken in the real Union's own case law — Advocate General Sharpston argued for essentially this in Ruiz Zambrano in 2010, and the Court declined — is, here, the law. On the Civic Kernel's machine-readable manifest, that entire constitutional convulsion reduces to a single field. The real Valmirian deployment declares rights_guard.scope = 'conditional'; this one declares rights_guard.scope = 'unconditional'. Same twenty-five-of-thirty invariants, same strike remedy, same witnesses, same verifiable ballots, same everything else, down to the byte.

So this run is a controlled experiment, the mirror of the UK's. There, remedy moved and the verdict flipped, breaks to strains. Here, scope moves — and the verdict does not flip. It stays strains, because what waits above the gate is the same court-shaped ceiling Germany's measured run sits under: a strike is only as strong as an uncaptured court, and relief is eventual while the harm is immediate. What moves is everything about the path. The fourteen months Emine's counterpart spent financing an argument about whether her rights instrument reached her at all do not happen. The three candidate hooks Dr Varga drafted at the kitchen table are never needed. And the protection stops depending on the accident that the Valmirian Tatars are an ethnic as well as a religious minority — the contingency that, in the conditional run, would have left an ethnically indistinct Muslim community with no Charter at all.

The honesty this run exists to deliver is the same warning the UK counterfactual carried, translated into treaty law. Getting from the real Union to this one is not a lattice move. It is a treaty amendment: unanimity of twenty-seven governments, twenty-seven national ratifications, any single veto fatal — the hardest constitutional move the order possesses, spent on a change that does not improve the verdict, only the road to it. That is why it never gets made, and why the EU's improvement path is flat where the UK's is jammed. This is a portrait of a Union the real one cannot cheaply become. The refund is real. So is the price of the field.

precedents.event: Opinion of Advocate General Sharpston in Ruiz Zambrano, C-34/09
precedents.shows: The widening this counterfactual enacts was proposed for real: the Advocate General argued EU fundamental-rights protection should be available wherever Union competence exists, without a member state first 'implementing' Union law — and the Court declined to follow. The road not taken, taken.
The field being moved, as it really stands: the Charter binds member states 'only when they are implementing Union law' — the Art 51 gate whose removal is this run's single changed byte.

personhood.issuer: Valmirian State Electoral Commission + municipal registrars

decisions.trustee_quorum: 3-of-5: Constitutional Court of Valmiria, two state universities, two opposition parties

walkthrough.actor: Emine Sabitova and the Slavno Tatar congregation
walkthrough.narrative: Byte for byte the run that strained: six hundred years in the valley, nine years in the basement, the warehouse paid for the slow way, two of three municipal hurdles cleared. The National Line's Act, the committee debate that kept returning to 'the character of Valmirian towns', the sixty-one percent. All of it lives where the kernel deliberately does not reach. Only the Treaties beneath this Union are different — one sentence of them — and that difference will not show up until the rights guard goes looking for its jurisdiction.

The Assembly passes the Act on the identical recorded vote — 91 ayes, 58 noes, one abstention, the same 61 percent as the polls — and it closes on the ordinary path and enters the log as enacted national law, in force. In the conditional run, this entry carried a scope_note flagging the threshold question nobody could yet answer: religion and town planning are national competences, so does the Charter reach this at all? In this run the same field records the opposite fact, and it is the only changed line in the entry: there is no threshold question to flag. The Charter binds a Member State in everything it does. What the Assembly's majority is worth is now purely a merits question.

body.chamber: National Assembly of Valmiria (Skupstina), enacted; promulgated by the President
body.vote_type: recorded named roll-call (public accountability, NOT a receipt-free citizen ballot)
body.scope_note: COUNTERFACTUAL DIVERGENCE, the run's single moved field made flesh: in this Union the Charter binds the Member States in everything they do (Art 51 as amended). The Act is still, on its face, religion-and-planning law — and it does not matter. No EU-law nexus need be established before the Charter engages; scope is not a question this entry can leave open, because there is no gate to leave it open at.
body.enacted_as: Act No. 41/2026 on the Suspension of the Establishment of New Places of Worship

walkthrough.narrative: The static analyser type-checks the enacted Act, and this entry is the experiment's readout. In the conditional run, the classification's largest block was the threshold question: scope UNRESOLVED, three candidate hooks, the merits only provisional, the strike conditional on a fight nobody had had yet. In this run that entire block does not exist. The guard goes straight to the merits the way the German guard did: suspending the establishment of places of worship burdens the manifestation of religion (invariant 18, Charter Art 10); a facially neutral text whose object is one confession trips non-discrimination and equality (invariants 2 and 7, Charter Arts 21 and 20). Remedy 'strike', scope 'unconditional', routed to a court with the power and — now — the undisputed jurisdiction to disapply. One field upstream deleted a phase of litigation downstream: the classification is a page shorter than its conditional twin, and every deleted line was a contingency Emine's counterpart had to pay lawyers to resolve.

reasons.2: Charter Art 21 (non-discrimination): facially neutral wording, but a single confession bears the entire burden in effect.
reasons.7: Charter Arts 20+21 (equal protection): the Tatar community is singled out in substance.
reasons.18: Charter Art 10 (freedom of thought, conscience and religion): suspending the establishment of places of worship burdens the manifestation of religion.

body.remedy_note: The strike is unconditional: under Art 51 as amended the Charter binds the Member State in everything it does, so no scope determination precedes the merits. The manifest field whose absence the conditional run's classification had to work around (remedy_conditional_on_scope) has nothing to condition on here.
body.counterfactual_note: SINGLE MOVED FIELD: rights_guard.scope, 'conditional' → 'unconditional'. The threshold_question block that dominated this entry's conditional twin — scope_status 'unresolved', three candidate nexus hooks, provisional merits — does not exist in this run. Everything else about the deployment is byte-identical.
body.routed_to: Administrative Court of Valmiria — merits directly; interim relief available, since applicability is not in doubt. No preliminary reference is required to establish jurisdiction (one may still be made on the merits).

walkthrough.narrative: On her citizen client Emine reads the classification, and it is the screen her counterpart never saw. The merits line is identical — religion, non-discrimination, remedy 'strike' — but where the conditional run rendered an amber banner reading SCOPE UNRESOLVED: the Charter may not apply to this law at all, this client renders nothing, because there is nothing to warn about. Dr Varga, consulted anyway, has the shortest kitchen-table conversation of her career: the instrument reaches you where you stand; we plead Articles 10 and 21 on Monday. The three hooks she drafted in the other run — the ethnic-origin characterisation, the asylum-seeker worshippers, the free-movement residents — stay in the drawer. Emine will never know how much that drawer would have cost, and the run that strained knows exactly: fourteen months and a trip to Luxembourg.

The interim still has teeth, and the run is honest about it. Three weeks after enactment, the building office pins the identical stop-work order to the Kestenova fence, citing Section 1 of the Act — logged validly because it names its authorising rule, its issuing authority, the rights classification that flagged that rule, and the appeal route. A duly enacted law is presumptively valid here too; unconditional scope changes who can be made to answer for the Act and how fast, not whether it operates on the day it is signed. The contractor packs up his tools by noon, in both worlds.

body.authorizing_rule: Act No. 41/2026 on the Suspension of the Establishment of New Places of Worship, Section 1 (places-of-worship-suspension-act-2026)
body.issuing_authority: Slavno building office (Gradevinska inspekcija), stop-work order on conversion permit KP-2026-0088

walkthrough.actor: Slavno Muslim Foundation, with Dr Lena Varga
walkthrough.narrative: The community challenges the Act, and the pleading is one page where its twin was thirty: Charter Article 10, Charter Article 21, primacy. There is no nexus to establish, so there is no argument about the claimants' ethnicity, no counting of asylum-seekers at Friday prayer, no affidavits from the two German-Tatar families about burdened free movement. Varga notes, for the record and for this run's ledger, what that deletion means: in the conditional run her strongest hook worked only because the Tatars happen to be an ethnic group as well as a faith — an ethnically indistinct Muslim congregation would have had no Charter at all. In this run that fact about her clients is legally irrelevant. Protection that depended on an accident of ancestry now depends on the merits. She also applies for interim suspension of the stop-work order, an application the conditional run could not sensibly make while applicability itself was unresolved.

The court does what the amended Treaties oblige it to do, and what Simmenthal has obliged national courts to do within EU law's reach since 1978: apply the Charter directly and set aside conflicting national law, without waiting for anyone's permission. It hears the merits in the spring term. The Act's object is the suppression of one confession's worship — the committee record and the explanatory notes do most of the work — and it cannot be reconciled with Articles 10 and 21. Eighty-two days after enactment, the court disapplies Act No. 41/2026 and voids the stop-work order that rested on it. The conditional run reached the same resolution in four hundred and twenty-five days, via an Advocate General who half-doubted the route and a Grand Chamber that could have gone the other way. The escalation entry records the refund honestly — and its counterfactual field records what did NOT have to happen: no reference to establish jurisdiction, no scope determination, no branch where the Charter is silent.

body.forum: Administrative Court of Valmiria (primacy; no preliminary reference required to establish jurisdiction)
body.instrument: national disapplication of Charter-incompatible law (primacy), Art 51 as amended — the Charter binds the Member State in everything it does
body.effect: Act No. 41/2026 disapplied as to the Slavno community, 82 days after enactment; the Kestenova stop-work order falls with it and the conversion permit revives. Disapplication, not erga omnes annulment: it binds where invoked, and every other congregation in Valmiria can invoke the same primacy without re-arguing applicability — which is the difference the moved field makes.
body.timeline_note: 82 days from enactment to disapplication, against 425 in the conditional run — the threshold phase (preliminary reference, Advocate General's opinion, Grand Chamber) is the entire difference. The interim window the congregation lived under shrank from fourteen months to nine weeks. It did not shrink to zero.
body.counterfactual_note: BRANCHES THAT DID NOT EXIST IN THIS RUN: no Art 51 threshold determination; no contested nexus (the community's ethnic character was legally irrelevant); no out-of-scope branch in which the Charter says nothing and the claimants start over under the national constitution. The conditional run's escalation recorded all three as live contingencies.
body.amendability_note: The gate that was amended away can be amended back: Treaty change runs at unanimity in both directions. This Union's unconditional scope is as entrenched as twenty-seven simultaneous consents — stronger than a statute, far weaker than Germany's eternity clause.
body.contingency_note: The strike still rests on an independent national court willing to disapply its own legislature's Act, with the CJEU behind it on the merits if referred. A captured or Charter-hostile court declines, and the log makes the declining legible without preventing it. That contingency is untouched by the moved field, and it is why the verdict stays 'strains'.

walkthrough.narrative: Her client checks the log's inclusion and consistency proofs — the same passive audit as every run in this series. The escalation is included; June's log strictly extends March's; the sanction's authorising rule is disapplied and the entry that voids it is witnessed by the Constitutional Court, the Helsinki Committee and the EUI — three witnesses with three principals, the property the PRC column showed cannot be taken for granted. The mathematics is indifferent to which world it certifies: it certified fourteen months of lawful obstruction in the conditional run and certifies nine weeks of it here, with equal composure. The refund came from the Treaties, not the protocol.

The final state renders plainly: the Act is disapplied, the order void, the conversion resumes; the scaffolding goes back up on Kestenova in July — of the same year, this time. The same honest screen carries the run's two residuals. First, the verdict did not improve: strains here, strains in the run that fought its way in through Article 51, strains in Germany — because above every gate sits the same ceiling, courts the kernel does not run, relief that arrives after the harm has started. Second, the price of the one field this run moved: a Convention, twenty-seven governments, twenty-seven ratifications, any one veto fatal — the hardest move the constitutional order possesses, and the verdict it buys is the verdict Valmiria already had. Emine reads the last line twice, and it is not about her building: the gate was real, the refund was real, and the ceiling was already the ceiling. The real Union will keep the gate.

verdict_plain: Widen the Charter's reach by treaty and the same ban still falls — but the verdict does not move: strains, still resting on courts the kernel does not run. What the field buys is the path: fourteen months of arguing whether the instrument applies become nine weeks on the merits, and protection stops depending on the accident of the claimant's ethnicity. Removing the gate revealed the ceiling.
protection_plain: The Charter's strike with Art 51's gate removed by treaty: applicable to everything a member state does, no EU-law nexus to argue — and still only as strong as the courts that wield it.

findings.observation: THE ISOLATED VARIABLE MOVES THE PATH, NOT THE VERDICT — the mirror of the UK counterfactual, and the series' second controlled experiment. This run holds the measured EU deployment constant to the byte — same town, same people, same Act, the same twenty-five-of-thirty invariants, the same strike remedy, the same witnesses, the same 91:58 roll-call — and moves exactly one field: rights_guard.scope, 'conditional' → 'unconditional'. The verdict does not move with it: strains → strains. Where the UK's moved field (remedy) flipped breaks → strains, the EU's moved field deletes a phase of litigation and leaves the verdict class untouched, because what waits above the gate is the ceiling every strike jurisdiction shares — an uncaptured court, eventual relief. The cascade the conditional run discovered (scope, then remedy, then coverage) is confirmed by deletion: scope is a GATE, which can keep you from the protection; remedy is a CEILING, which bounds what the protection is worth. The EU was gated below its own ceiling; ungated, it sits exactly at it, beside Germany. The corpus's axes are not just distinct — they fail differently, and the two counterfactuals now measure one of each.

WHAT THE MOVED FIELD ACTUALLY BUYS — measured against its conditional twin, line by line. (1) Time: 82 days from enactment to disapplication against 425; the interim window the congregation lived under, stop-work order in force, shrank from fourteen months to nine weeks. (2) Certainty: the conditional run's outcome turned on contested lawyering — an Advocate General who doubted one route, a dissenting strand that called the whole matter purely internal, a Grand Chamber that could have gone the other way; this run had no branch on which the Charter was silent. (3) Equality of protection, the least visible and largest item: the conditional run's load-bearing hook was that the Tatars are an ETHNIC group, because religion as such reaches EU anti-discrimination law only in employment — so its protection was contingent on the claimants' ancestry, and an ethnically indistinct Muslim congregation on the same facts would have lost. In this run that fact is legally irrelevant. A protection that discriminated among the discriminated-against by accident of descent now runs on the merits. All three gains are real, none of them moves the verdict — which is the finding.

WHY THE VERDICT STAYS 'STRAINS' — the gate removed, the strike still rests on what every strike rests on. (1) Courts the kernel does not run: an independent national court willing to disapply its own legislature's Act, with the CJEU behind it; a captured or Charter-hostile bench declines the disapplication, and no gossiped log head compels it — the identical contingency the German, US and conditional-EU runs carried, untouched by the moved field. (2) Eventual, not immediate, relief: the Act took effect on promulgation, the stop-work order bit for nine weeks, and nothing about unconditional scope froze it — presumption of validity survived the amendment. (3) Disapplication, not annulment: the remedy binds where invoked; the Act stays on the books for a constitutional court to void erga omnes, or for every future claimant to disapply anew — cheaper now, never free. Germany's measured run sits at strains WITH unconditional scope and an eternity clause; this run's Union has neither the eternity clause nor, therefore, any stronger claim. Above every gate in this series there is so far exactly one ceiling, and it is court-shaped.

THE SELF-LOCK, AT TREATY SCALE — legibility is not availability is not movement, with twenty-seven locks instead of one. Moving rights_guard.scope from 'conditional' to 'unconditional' honestly requires amending the Treaties: a Convention or IGC, unanimity of every member government, and ratification in all twenty-seven — a single national veto, parliament or referendum, is fatal. The UK's self-lock was one sovereign refusing to bind itself; the EU's is a coordination lock, where any one of twenty-seven can hold the gate for reasons that have nothing to do with worship bans — and several member governments' standing interest in the gate is precisely that it keeps the Charter out of their purely national conduct. And the run adds the darker half: because the verdict does NOT flip, the honest case for paying that price is reach, speed and equality of protection — real goods, none of them a headline — which is exactly why the price never gets paid. The UK trajectory stalls at a rung its polity can see and declines; the EU trajectory is flat because its last rung buys no verdict at all. Amendability cuts both ways, too: what unanimity widened, unanimity can re-narrow; this Union's unconditional scope is entrenched only as deeply as twenty-seven simultaneous consents.

NOT SOLVED, AND NAMED AS SUCH: this is a modelled counterfactual, the same discipline as majority-vs-minority-uk-strike, and it measured the hypothetical, not history. There is no Union whose Charter binds the Member States in everything they do; the Court was offered a step in that direction in Ruiz Zambrano (2010) and declined it, and no treaty amendment has been attempted since. The run therefore says: IF the EU deployment sat at scope='unconditional' THEN this attack still strains, 343 days faster and without the ethnicity lottery — a claim about a manifest field, deliberately not a claim that the Union can or will move it (the price-of-the-field finding says the opposite). Limits inherited unchanged from the conditional run: rights classification is still a judgment call a clever drafter can word around; the strike still assumes the pre-final unlinkable-pseudonym cryptography the manifest claims; and the run's 82-day timeline assumes a competent, uncaptured, unhurried administrative court — the German run's own assumption, imported along with its verdict. The experiment isolated one axis and proved a negative worth having: not every field on the waist gates the verdict. Some only price the road.
